libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that exercise reflective/private member access, configure the Gradle Test JVM with --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED and --add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED. Do not set ognl.security.manager globally; if sandbox/security-manager behavior is tested, set and restore the property inside the specific test, preferably before isolated OgnlRuntime initialization.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: JDK 25 may further restrict or remove SecurityManager behavior, so tests should tolerate unsupported System.setSecurityManager paths rather than depending on successful installation.

OGNL's expression compiler uses javassist dynamic class generation; native-image coverage may need to avoid or explicitly handle unsupported dynamic classloading paths, but this does not require an extra dependency because javassist is transitive.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is an in-process expression/reflection library and its only runtime dependency, javassist, is declared transitively by ognl:ognl:3.3.5, so no extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed. However upstream 3.3.5 test configuration uses JDK module opens for reflective access, which meaningful OGNL member-access coverage may need.
